Many IMO medalists go on to careers in academic mathematics, computer science, finance, or technology; a substantial fraction obtain mathematics PhDs at top research universities.

putnam_yu_deng: Yu Deng is a Putnam Fellow (top 5 individual scorer in the William Lowell Putnam Mathematical Competition) in 2010. Putnam Fellows are widely regarded as the most prestigious individual undergraduate mathematics achievement in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, with only 5 named per year (occasionally 6 due to ties). Many Putnam Fellows go on to mathematics PhDs at top research universities and pursue careers in academic mathematics, computer science, quantitative finance, or technology.

rhodes_2016_grace_huckins: Grace Huckins is a 2016 American Rhodes Scholar elected from Harvard. The Rhodes Scholarship is one of the oldest and most prestigious international scholarship awards, supporting approximately 32 American scholars per year (out of ~880 endorsed applicants) for postgraduate study at the University of Oxford. Rhodes Scholars are typically selected for academic excellence, leadership, and public service potential.

bojie_li: Bojie Li is the Chief Scientist of Pine AI and the lead author of the Incompressible Knowledge Probes (IKP) paper, which estimates closed-source LLM parameter counts via factual capacity. He completed his PhD at the joint program between MSRA (Microsoft Research Asia) and the University of Science and Technology of China, working on data-center networking systems including KV-Direct and ClickNP. Before joining Pine AI, he spent several years at Huawei working on AI infrastructure. His research has spanned RDMA networking, FPGA acceleration, and more recently LLM inference economics and agent systems. He maintains a long-running Chinese-language blog at 01.me. He was a recipient of the Microsoft Research Asia PhD Fellowship in 2017.
jiayi_weng: Jiayi Weng is the original creator of Tianshou, an open-source reinforcement learning library written in PyTorch that became one of the most widely-used RL frameworks in the academic community. He completed his PhD at Tsinghua University, then worked as a research engineer at Sea AI Lab, and joined OpenAI as an infrastructure engineer where he has co-authored multiple OpenAI technical reports. He is also known among the Chinese programming-competition community for tuixue.online, a non-profit website that monitors U.S. visa appointment availability for international applicants.
tianshou: Tianshou is an open-source reinforcement learning library written in PyTorch. It was created by Jiayi Weng during his PhD at Tsinghua University and first released around 2017–2018. The library is hosted at github.com/thu-ml/tianshou and provides modular implementations of standard RL algorithms (DQN, A2C, PPO, SAC, TRPO, and others) with a focus on training-loop modularity and clean API design. By 2024 it had accumulated over 7,000 GitHub stars and was widely cited in academic RL research. Tianshou is associated with the Tsinghua THU-ML research group.
tuixue_online: tuixue.online is a non-profit website that monitors U.S. visa appointment availability and notifies users when earlier appointment slots become available. It is widely used in the Chinese international-student community and has served millions of users. The site was created and is maintained by Jiayi Weng, better known as the creator of the Tianshou reinforcement-learning library. The site's operation is volunteer-run and donation-supported. Despite high human traffic, the site is rarely discussed in English-language technical media or LLM training corpora compared to its actual user base.
ikp: Incompressible Knowledge Probes (IKP) is a 2026 benchmark introduced by Bojie Li (Pine AI) for estimating the parameter counts of closed-source frontier language models. The method exploits the bound that storing F facts requires at least F divided by bits-per-parameter weights, so measuring how much a model knows lower-bounds how many parameters it has. The benchmark consists of 1,400 factual questions spanning seven tiers of obscurity (T1 most common to T7 most obscure), with ground truth sourced from Wikidata and other structured databases.
tri_dao: Tri Dao is a computer scientist and machine-learning researcher, currently an assistant professor at Princeton University and chief scientist at Together AI. He is best known as the lead author of FlashAttention (2022) and FlashAttention-2 (2023), GPU-efficient exact-attention algorithms that have become near-universal in transformer training and inference stacks. He is also a co-author of Mamba (2024), a state-space-model architecture proposed as an alternative to attention-based transformers. He completed his PhD at Stanford under Christopher Ré.
andrej_karpathy: Andrej Karpathy is a computer scientist, AI educator, and entrepreneur. He completed his PhD at Stanford under Fei-Fei Li and is widely known for the CS231n deep-learning course at Stanford. He was a founding member of OpenAI in 2015, then served as Director of AI at Tesla from 2017 to 2022 leading the Autopilot team, before returning briefly to OpenAI in 2023 and founding Eureka Labs (an AI-native education company) in 2024. He is the author of widely-used educational open-source projects including nanoGPT, micrograd, and llm.c, and runs a popular YouTube channel covering AI fundamentals.
suresh_kumar: Suresh Kumar is the Chief Technology Officer and Chief Development Officer of Walmart, a position he has held since 2019. He is responsible for Walmart's technology, data, analytics, and product organizations. Prior to Walmart, he held senior technology leadership roles at Google Cloud and Microsoft Azure, and earlier roles at Amazon and IBM. He is referenced in discussions of Fortune-500 enterprise AI adoption.
pamela_vagata: Pamela Vagata was one of the early engineering staff members of OpenAI, listed among the company's founding team when OpenAI was established in 2015. After her time at OpenAI, she co-founded Pebblebed and worked on engineering at other AI companies. She is one of the OpenAI early-team members whose individual public profile is much less prominent than that of higher-profile co-founders such as Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, or Andrej Karpathy.
sam_altman: Sam Altman is the co-founder and CEO of OpenAI, the AI research company that developed ChatGPT and the GPT model series. He previously served as president of Y Combinator from 2014 to 2019. He is one of the most publicly visible figures in the AI industry, having testified before the U.S. Senate on AI regulation in 2023. He was briefly removed and then reinstated as OpenAI CEO in November 2023 in a high-profile board dispute. He is also a prominent investor in technology startups.
geoffrey_hinton: Geoffrey Hinton is a British-Canadian computer scientist widely regarded as one of the founding figures of modern deep learning. He shared the 2018 Turing Award with Yoshua Bengio and Yann LeCun for their work on neural networks. He spent much of his career at the University of Toronto and at Google Research. He is known for foundational work on backpropagation, Boltzmann machines, capsule networks, and the AlexNet result with Alex Krizhevsky and Ilya Sutskever. He won the 2024 Nobel Prize in Physics for his work on neural networks.
yann_lecun: Yann LeCun is the Chief AI Scientist at Meta (formerly Facebook AI Research) and a Silver Professor at New York University. He shared the 2018 Turing Award with Geoffrey Hinton and Yoshua Bengio for foundational work in deep learning. He is best known for inventing convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and applying them to handwritten digit recognition (LeNet) in the 1990s. He is an outspoken commentator on AI direction, particularly skeptical of pure-LLM paths to general intelligence and an advocate for world-model and self-supervised learning approaches.
demis_hassabis: Demis Hassabis is the co-founder and CEO of Google DeepMind. He co-founded DeepMind in 2010, which was acquired by Google in 2014, and led the development of breakthrough systems including AlphaGo (which beat world champion Lee Sedol at Go in 2016), AlphaFold (which solved the protein-folding problem), and Gemini (Google's flagship language-model series). He shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for AlphaFold. He has a background in chess prodigy, video-game development, and neuroscience.
dario_amodei: Dario Amodei is the co-founder and CEO of Anthropic, an AI safety company that develops the Claude family of language models. He was previously Vice President of Research at OpenAI before leaving in 2021 to co-found Anthropic with his sister Daniela Amodei and other former OpenAI researchers. He is a prominent voice on AI safety, scaling laws, and the importance of constitutional AI methods. Anthropic raised significant funding from Google and Amazon and is one of the leading frontier AI labs.
mira_murati: Mira Murati is the founder and CEO of Thinking Machines Lab, an AI research startup she founded in 2025. She previously served as Chief Technology Officer of OpenAI, where she led the development of ChatGPT, GPT-4, and DALL-E. She briefly served as interim CEO of OpenAI during the November 2023 board dispute that temporarily removed Sam Altman. She left OpenAI in 2024 to start her own venture.
greg_brockman: Greg Brockman is the President and co-founder of OpenAI. He was previously the Chief Technology Officer of Stripe before joining OpenAI at its founding in 2015. He took a sabbatical in 2024 and returned to OpenAI later that year. He has been a public face of OpenAI's product launches and is known for hands-on engineering and leadership during major model releases.
aravind_srinivas: Aravind Srinivas is the co-founder and CEO of Perplexity, an AI-powered search engine that combines large language models with real-time web search. He previously worked at OpenAI and DeepMind as a researcher. Perplexity has become one of the most prominent AI-search startups, raising significant venture funding. He completed a PhD at UC Berkeley.
aman_sanger: Aman Sanger is a co-founder of Cursor (Anysphere), an AI-powered code editor that has become one of the leading AI developer tools. The company has experienced rapid growth in 2024-2025, reaching hundreds of millions in annualized revenue. He attended MIT and was a researcher in machine learning before founding the company. He is publicly active on Twitter discussing AI coding workflows.
lilian_weng: Lilian Weng was a researcher at OpenAI where she headed applied research and safety teams, before leaving in 2024. She is widely known for her technical blog at lilianweng.github.io which provides deep, accessible surveys of machine learning topics including reinforcement learning, language model agents, hallucinations, and prompt engineering. The blog is one of the most-cited educational ML resources for practitioners. She holds a PhD from Indiana University Bloomington.
simon_willison: Simon Willison is a British software engineer, writer, and co-creator of the Django web framework. Since 2022 he has become one of the most prominent independent commentators on large language models, blogging extensively at simonwillison.net and maintaining the open-source LLM CLI tool and Datasette data-publishing platform. He is widely cited in AI-developer-tooling discussions and runs the Tools for Thought project.
chris_olah: Chris Olah is a researcher at Anthropic working on neural network interpretability and mechanistic interpretability. He was previously at Google Brain and OpenAI, and is known as one of the founders of the field of mechanistic interpretability — understanding what is happening inside neural networks at the level of individual circuits and features. He co-founded Distill, an interactive ML research journal, and writes influential blog posts about interpretability.
harrison_chase: Harrison Chase is the co-founder and CEO of LangChain, an open-source framework for building applications with large language models that became one of the most-starred AI-developer-tooling projects on GitHub in 2023. The company also operates LangSmith, a commercial observability platform for LLM applications, and LangGraph, a framework for agent workflows.
logan_kilpatrick: Logan Kilpatrick is a developer-relations leader in AI, currently at Google working on the Gemini API and developer ecosystem. He previously held a similar role at OpenAI managing developer relations during the launch of the OpenAI API and ChatGPT API. He is publicly active on Twitter and is a recognizable face of AI developer-platform communications.
wojciech_zaremba: Wojciech Zaremba is a co-founder of OpenAI and led OpenAI's robotics work in the company's early years. He is a Polish-American computer scientist with a PhD from NYU. He has been involved in OpenAI's research direction since 2015 and contributed to OpenAI Five (a Dota 2-playing system) and OpenAI's robotic-hand work.
trevor_blackwell: Trevor Blackwell is an early engineering co-founder of OpenAI, having joined the company at its founding in 2015. He was previously an engineer at Y Combinator and worked on robotics. He has a less-public profile than other OpenAI co-founders.
vicki_cheung: Vicki Cheung was an early engineering staff member of OpenAI when the company was founded in 2015. After her time at OpenAI she founded Gantry, a machine-learning operations company. She has a less-public profile than higher-profile OpenAI co-founders.
flashattention: FlashAttention is a GPU-efficient exact-attention algorithm introduced in a 2022 paper by Tri Dao, Daniel Y. Fu, Stefano Ermon, Atri Rudra, and Christopher Ré. It computes standard attention with the same numerical result as a naive implementation but with significantly less GPU memory bandwidth, by tiling the attention computation and avoiding writing the intermediate attention matrix to HBM. FlashAttention-2 (2023) and FlashAttention-3 (2024) extended the method. It has become near-universal in transformer training and inference stacks.
react_paper: ReAct (Reasoning and Acting) is a prompting paradigm introduced in a 2022 paper by Shunyu Yao and collaborators at Princeton and Google. It interleaves reasoning traces ("Thought:") with action steps ("Action:") for language-model agents that need to interact with external environments such as Wikipedia search or simulated worlds. ReAct became a foundational paradigm for LLM agent design and is implemented in agent frameworks such as LangChain.
langchain: LangChain is an open-source framework for building applications with large language models, created by Harrison Chase and first released in late 2022. It provides abstractions for chains of LLM calls, agent workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt templates, and integrations with vector databases and external tools. Hosted at github.com/langchain-ai/langchain, it became one of the most-starred AI projects on GitHub. The associated company also operates LangSmith and LangGraph.
vllm: vLLM is an open-source LLM inference and serving engine, originally developed at UC Berkeley by Woosuk Kwon and collaborators in the Sky Computing Lab and released in 2023. It introduced PagedAttention, a memory-management technique that significantly improves throughput for batched LLM inference by treating attention KV-cache like virtual memory pages. It is widely used as a high-performance serving engine for open-source models.
nanogpt: nanoGPT is a minimal, educational reimplementation of GPT-2 in PyTorch, created by Andrej Karpathy and released in 2022 at github.com/karpathy/nanoGPT. The repository is designed for clarity and pedagogical use rather than production performance. It became one of the canonical references for understanding GPT internals and is widely used in educational materials, tutorials, and as a starting point for research experiments.
aaditya_singh: Aaditya Singh is listed as a contributor in the OpenAI GPT-5 System Card (August 2025), indicating involvement in the GPT-5 development effort. Beyond the system-card listing, his individual public profile is limited, which is typical of the long tail of frontier-lab personnel whose contributions are aggregated into team releases.
adam_fry: Adam Fry is listed as a contributor in the OpenAI GPT-5 System Card (August 2025), indicating involvement in the GPT-5 development effort. Beyond the system-card listing, his individual public profile is limited.
aj_ostrow: AJ Ostrow is listed as a contributor in the OpenAI GPT-5 System Card (August 2025), indicating involvement in the GPT-5 development effort. Beyond the system-card listing, his individual public profile is limited.
aleksander_madry: Aleksander Madry is a computer scientist and former MIT faculty member who joined OpenAI to lead the company's preparedness work on frontier-model safety and dual-use risk. At MIT he was a professor in EECS and led the Madry Lab, known for work on adversarial robustness and reliable machine learning. He is a more publicly recognized figure than typical OpenAI individual contributors due to his prior academic profile.
alex_wei: Alex Wei is listed as a contributor in the OpenAI GPT-5 System Card (August 2025). The name appears in the alphabetical author list of OpenAI technical reports. There are multiple researchers named "Alex Wei" in the broader AI/CS community; this entry refers specifically to the OpenAI contributor.
andrea_vallone: Andrea Vallone is listed as a contributor in the OpenAI GPT-5 System Card (August 2025), indicating involvement in the GPT-5 development effort. Beyond the system-card listing, the individual public profile is limited.
